cause: Accessibility text bounds, visual OCR ROI results, overlay retention, and YouTube scroll/mini-player lifecycle are not yet sharing one strict validity window.
change: Recorded as a separate failure before further Android masking changes so later commits can be judged against this exact symptom.
problem: User screenshot shows masks remaining on profile/channel metadata and thumbnail regions while visible harmful text can still remain after scrolling; the mini-player also overlaps the feed, increasing stale overlay risk.
remaining_risk: Need next Android loop to invalidate visual masks on active scroll/miniplayer transitions and only render masks tied to current viewport generation.
title: YouTube scroll and mini-player leave stale masks on wrong regions
verification: Manual evidence from 2026-05-17 user screenshot in Codex session 019e3546-cfae-7450-880d-006c2f1102d5; no code fix claimed by this entry.

cause: MaskOverlayController.translateBy returned the failed translation status before clearing active views, and the service marked overlay revision stale while preserving masks for unresolved scroll deltas.
change: translateBy now clears active overlay views when translation produces no specs; unresolved or unrenderable scroll translations clear the overlay immediately and schedule recapture.
problem: Scroll translation could return ALL_OFFSCREEN, NO_TRANSLATABLE_MASKS, or REJECTED_DELTA without removing the existing overlay views, leaving stale masks visible until a later recapture.
remaining_risk: This reduces stale wrong-position masks, but first-frame exposure and OCR latency still need runtime validation on an attached emulator/device.
title: Clear untranslatable masks during YouTube scroll recapture gaps
verification: Focused MaskOverlayPlannerTest/MaskOverlayEventPolicyTest passed; :app:testDebugUnitTest passed; :app:assembleDebug passed; git diff --check passed. No device was attached for APK install.

cause: Commit history, session history, and report evidence were serving overlapping roles; one session_id could not distinguish where a failure happened from where it was registered.
change: Extended the evidence ledger schema with source_session_id, registrar_session_id, evidence_quality, and report_ready; added docs/evidence/evidence-matrix.md to map model, parser, Android bounds, and E2E demo evidence to Notion cards and repo artifacts.
problem: Git history was becoming too deep while failures, experiments, and report evidence were not consistently recoverable across Codex sessions, repo docs, Notion, PRs, and Linear.
title: Split development source sessions from evidence registration
verification: python3 scripts/chungmaru_evidence.py validate passed; python3 scripts/chungmaru_evidence.py summarize passed; git diff --check passed.

cause: The existing model failure analysis JSON was useful but not directly shaped as a report-ready evidence artifact linked from the repo evidence ledger.
change: Added docs/evidence/model-evaluation-snapshot.md with 1007-case summary, precision/recall/F1, FP/FN counts, score bucket distribution, clean-topic bias counts, and 10 FP plus 10 FN representative cases.
problem: The capstone review checklist requires Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, FP, FN, score bucket errors, topic-bias checks, and FP/FN examples, but those facts were spread across local model artifacts and presentation notes.
title: Freeze current model evaluation baseline snapshot
verification: Parsed docs/Backend model/failure_analysis_20260506_171404.json to confirm TP 379, TN 412, FP 86, FN 130, Accuracy 78.55%, Precision 81.51%, Recall 74.46%, and F1 77.82%; python3 scripts/chungmaru_evidence.py validate passed.

cause: The parser fixtures and comparison script existed, but their outputs were not fixed as a report-ready artifact linked from the evidence ledger.
change: Added docs/evidence/parser-extractor-before-after.md with YouTube and Chrome raw/kept/removed tables, cleaning rule explanation, model input payload example, and remaining platform gaps.
problem: The review checklist requires raw JSONL vs cleaned JSONL examples, UI text removal rules, cleaned JSONL to model input flow, and Android boundsInScreen usage evidence.
title: Freeze parser raw-to-cleaned before-after fixtures
verification: Ran compare_snapshots.py for YouTube and Chrome fixtures; outputs showed filter chips, metadata, browser chrome removed while content candidates retained boundsInScreen.

cause: Worktrees isolate risky or long-running implementation work, but without ledger metadata the final evidence trail would not show which branch or worktree produced an observation.
change: Extended the evidence ledger with worktree_path, worktree_role, source_branch, and integration_branch; documented the policy in evidence README, reporting workflow, evidence matrix, and repo-local evidence capture skill.
problem: Development sessions will continue in parallel, so the official checkout can become dirty with Android/backend/extension experiments while report evidence and Notion tracking still need to stay coherent.
title: Adopt worktree isolation for ongoing development sessions
verification: python3 scripts/chungmaru_evidence.py audit-worktrees showed the current official checkout and dirty count; python3 -m py_compile scripts/chungmaru_evidence.py passed.

cause: Repo ledger, Git history, and Notion board serve different purposes; treating Notion as a full event log would hide the May 14 review checklist behind too many operational entries.
change: Documented that repo ledger remains append-only, Notion keeps curated representative issues, and each sync should select 3-5 focus items across model, parser/extractor, and integrated demo evidence.
problem: The Notion board can become noisy if every development event is copied there, while the capstone review needs a small set of representative model, parser, and end-to-end evidence items.
title: Keep full history in ledger and curate Notion around review focus
verification: Updated docs/evidence/evidence-matrix.md and docs/reporting-workflow.md with the 2026-05-14 review focus policy.
